items.title: Accused criminals flown out of remote communities as WA regional courts shut

children.content: WA Police have been forced to fly some accused criminals out of remote communities since the start of this month due to regional court closures sparked by a security dispute.

items.title: Tradie found dead by side of road 16 years ago was murdered, coroner finds

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

children.content: A coroner has found the investigation into a Broome tradies' murder, which remains an enduring mystery, was mishandled by WA Police.

items.title: Woman denied bail over alleged plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of WA

children.content: Queensland woman Jessica Beckett told a West Australian court she did not know an outlaw bikie gang member was on the boat she was sailing to Indonesia.

image.altText: Two people faced blurred standing on jetty

items.title: Mother of Perth girl shot dead in Pakistan 'in extreme shock'

children.content: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

image.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: 'Never crossed my mind he wouldn't survive': Seven die daily from drug overdose

children.content: Australia recorded its deadliest year on record for drug overdoses, according to a new analysis, with an average of seven deaths per day.

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale

items.title: RBA tipped to keep rates on hold as US-Iran deal sends stocks soaring

children.content: Wall Street rallied overnight, with the Dow closing at a fresh high. Here, the cash rate is widely forecast to remain unchanged at 4.35 per cent, after three consecutive rate hikes this year. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: WA travellers among those owed $4.3 million as dream holidays shattered

children.content: Perth retiree Paul Beard is just one of the Australians left thousands of dollars out of pocket after the collapse of AVG Travels, with the situation prompting calls for more protection for travel agency customers.

items.title: New watchdog proposed to probe behaviour of WA judges

children.content: Complaints about the behaviour and capacity of judges and magistrates will be investigated by an independent watchdog, under new legislation to be introduced to WA parliament this week.

items.title: With the success of 007 First Light, the future of James Bond is looking bright

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

items.title: Soccer tournament unites refugees through universal language of sport

children.content: A soccer tournament in Perth's north helps participants overcome barriers, while inspiring more women to pursue the game, as part of Refugee Week.
